metadata.title:
ASSEMBLING BUILDING TRADES


UNDER ONE DISTRICT HEAD

pages.raw_text:
The work of reassembling all of
spokane's building trades under
one head has almost been COM
pleted. The painters union was
taken into that department of the
American Federation of Labor last
night, and only a few more unions
entitled to affiliation wit hthe de-
partment, by merit of the bylaws
of the A. F. of L., renal noutside.
Even the plasterers, bricklayers
and masons, whose international
unions are not affiliated with the
American Federation of Labor,
hence not entitled to the protec-
ton Of the entire alliance in a
national labor movement, will like
ly take seat in the local alliance.


The work so far done shows a
greater success by far than was
expected when the work of reas-
sembling the building trades crafts
into one department was begun by
G. F. Staub a month ago.


The third meeting of the differ
ent unions was held last night for
the purpose Of getting together
again. All the IS unions entitled
to a seat in this department were
